title: Workflow Syntax, Expressions, YAML, and Data Contracts · Automation Case: Ci Context Availability Allowlist
summary: A workflow parses but triggers unexpectedly, treats false as text, expands a multiline output incorrectly, inherits a permissive default, and hides a misspelled key. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci context availability allowlist increment in validated workflow data contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select contexts only where the workflow syntax permits them and separate configuration-time from runner-time values.
Reason about expression literals, loose equality, coercion, truthiness, functions, object filters, fromJSON, and toJSON without leaking data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-context-availability-allowlist — Select contexts only where the workflow syntax permits them and separate configuration-time from runner-time values.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-workflow-syntax-expressions-5
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-workflow-syntax-expressions-5
    artifact: workflow-contract.yaml
    decision: worked gha workflow syntax expressions 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      name: verified-ci
      on-events: [pull_request, push]
      permissions: contents-read
      concurrency: revision-scoped
      expression-input: parsed-with-fromJSON
      shell-input: validated-environment
      schema: action-inputs-reviewed
      changed-event-fixture: fork-pull-request
    verify: validate workflow schema expressions context availability action inputs and changed event fixtures
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only review, implicit coercion, unavailable contexts, or direct untrusted expression interpolation.
    repair: Validate workflow semantics, parse explicit types, move untrusted values through data boundaries, and test changed payloads.
